meaning: n.(动物)触须、触角,(植物)腺毛
examples: Each tentacle is about two millimeters long.
Enthusiasts have been hospitalized after a wiggling tentacle lodged in the throat.
Scientists pulled up a tentacle and they believe it came from a foot-long jellyfish.
As far as I could tell, Idan was a glowy tentacle of energy that could knock over a can of soda or even open a hatch.
Second, the galleries of portraits reveal the extraordinary tentacle of the Habsburg family and the overweening preoccupation with dynastic succession and the demands of strategic alliances.
